body: import java.io.UnsupportedEncodingException;
import java.nio.charset.StandardCharsets;
import org.apache.commons.codec.DecoderException;
import org.apache.commons.codec.EncoderException;
import org.junit.Test;
import org.apache.commons.codec.CharEncoding;
import static org.junit.Assert.fail;
import static org.junit.Assert.assertEquals;
import static org.junit.Assert.assertNull;

translation: urlCodec.encode2(plain) # To work around a weird quirk in Java 1.2.2
self.assertIsNone("Encoding a null Object should return null", result)
self.assertEqual("Basic URL decoding test", plain, urlCodec.decode2(encoded))
self.assertIsNone(encoded, "Encoding a null string should return null")
self.assertEqual("Unsafe chars URL decoding test", plain, urlCodec.decode2(encoded))
self.assertEqual("Safe chars URL encoding test", plain, encoded)
self.assertEqual("Safe chars URL decoding test", plain, urlCodec.decode2(encoded))
self.assertEqual("Basic URL decoding test", plain, urlCodec.decode2(encoded))

body: private void validateState(final URLCodec urlCodec) {}
